segments.text: Now after these things, the Lord also appointed seventy others, and sent them two by two ahead of him into every city and place where he was about to come.
Then he said to them,
The harvest is indeed plentiful, but the laborers are few. Pray therefore to the Lord of the harvest, that he may send out laborers into his harvest.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Go your ways. Behold, I send you out as lambs among wolves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Carry no purse, nor wallet, nor sandals. Greet no one on the way.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Into whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Peace be to this house.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If a son of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; but if not, it will return to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Remain in that same house, eating and drinking the things they give, for the laborer is worthy of his wages. Don’t go from house to house.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Into whatever city you enter and they receive you, eat the things that are set before you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Heal the sick who are there and tell them, ‘God’s Kingdom has come near to you.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But into whatever city you enter and they don’t receive you, go out into its streets and say,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘Even the dust from your city that clings to us, we wipe off against you. Nevertheless know this, that God’s Kingdom has come near to you.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, it will be more tolerable in that day for Sodom than for that city.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, will be brought down to Hades.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever listens to you listens to me, and whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects him who sent me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The seventy returned with joy, saying,
Lord, even the demons are subject to us in your name!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to them,
I saw Satan having fallen like lightning from heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Behold, I give you authority to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will in any way hurt you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Nevertheless, don’t rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In that same hour, Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit, and said,

I thank you, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for so it was well-pleasing in your sight.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Turning to the disciples, he said,
All things have been delivered to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is, except the Father, and who the Father is, except the Son, and he to whomever the Son desires to reveal him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Turning to the disciples, he said privately,
Blessed are the eyes which see the things that you see,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: for I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see the things which you see, and didn’t see them, and to hear the things which you hear, and didn’t hear them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Behold, a certain lawyer stood up and tested him, saying,
Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?
segments.speaker: lawyer

segments.text: He said to him,
What is written in the law? How do you read it?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He answered,
You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your strength, and with all your mind; and your neighbor as yourself.
segments.speaker: lawyer

segments.text: He said to him,
You have answered correctly. Do this, and you will live.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But he, desiring to justify himself, asked Jesus,
Who is my neighbor?
segments.speaker: he, desiring to justify himself,

segments.text: Jesus answered,
A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and he fell among robbers, who both stripped him and beat him, and departed, leaving him half dead.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: By chance a certain priest was going down that way. When he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the same way a Levite also, when he came to the place and saw him, passed by on the other side.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But a certain Samaritan, as he traveled, came where he was. When he saw him, he was moved with compassion,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: came to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. He set him on his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: On the next day, when he departed, he took out two denarii, gave them to the host, and said to him, ‘Take care of him. Whatever you spend beyond that, I will repay you when I return.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now which of these three do you think seemed to be a neighbor to him who fell among the robbers?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said,
He who showed mercy on him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus said to him,
Go and do likewise.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As they went on their way, he entered into a certain village, and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.
She had a sister called Mary, who also sat at Jesus’ feet and heard his word.
But Martha was distracted with much serving, and she came up to him, and said,
Lord, don’t you care that my sister left me to serve alone? Ask her therefore to help me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus answered her,
Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about many things,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but one thing is needed. Mary has chosen the good part, which will not be taken away from her.
segments.speaker: Jesus
